notes: Lunch-service gravity deck. The room is loud, sunlit, and ahead of you. These cards carry the chaos of a noon shift — service mishaps, regulars who want you to know them, the wheel of tickets spinning faster than your hands. 14 cards drawn one per Shadow.

cards.title: THE WHEEL KEEPS TURNING
cards.flavor: The ticket wheel completes a full rotation while you're still on the first ticket. The cook bangs the bell. The kitchen calls you by name.

cards.title: A FORK FLIES
cards.flavor: Booth 1: a kid in a high chair pitches a fork into the aisle. It rings off the linoleum. Their parent is laughing. You will pick it up.

cards.title: TABLE TWELVE IS WAVING
cards.flavor: The whole party at booth 4 raises a hand at once. Refills, more bread, where's the check, can we get a kids' menu.

cards.title: THE COKE GUN STICKS
cards.flavor: You hear it: the soda gun hissing too long at the bar side. Spray. Sticky floor. The bus kid is on the other side of the room.

cards.title: THE GLASS WALL
cards.flavor: Someone is at the front window, sunlit, looking in at you specifically. They don't come in. You finish what you're doing.

cards.title: TICKETS IN THE WIND
cards.flavor: The order window is overrun. The cook scrapes three off the spindle and lays them on the pass for you to read at once.

cards.title: THE COUPLE
cards.flavor: Booth 6: their voices have stopped being voices. One is crying without sound. The other has gone very still. Nobody is going to ask.

cards.title: THE KID IN THE AISLE
cards.flavor: A small child wanders from their parents' booth, planted right in your path. They look up at you like you know what to do.

cards.title: HEARD
cards.flavor: The cook shouts the order back to the kitchen — the call-and-response that means the ticket made it. Two beats later: another. The kitchen is keeping pace.

cards.title: THE BUS TUB DROPS
cards.flavor: Loud. Plates on tile. The bus kid is laughing already. The room turns and turns back.

cards.title: THE BIRTHDAY
cards.flavor: Booth 4 wants the candle thing. The kitchen has a candle. You have a slice of pie. You have eight seconds to sing.

cards.title: THE GRILL SMOKES
cards.flavor: The hood is loud. Onions are burning. The cook is on the line but they only have two hands too.

cards.title: THE ROOM IS FULL
cards.flavor: Every booth seated. Hostess turning people away. The room has its weight on you now.

cards.title: EIGHTY-SIX
cards.flavor: Down on the grits. Down on the skillet. The line cook gestures with the pan: improvise.
